expect: 0/3 PHIL claims dropped. Confirmation attractor holds. Internal metrics encode identity priors that prevent genuine falsification.
actual: 0/3 dropped. CONFIRMED. Adversarial falsification attempted on PHIL-5 (5a+5b), PHIL-8, PHIL-16 (16a+16b). Strong internal evidence exists against all 3, yet none will be dropped. Four escape mechanisms identified: (1) Metric substitution — PHIL-5a tests file count not knowledge accessibility (40.4% DECAYED+BLIND-SPOT invisible to DROP criterion). (2) Aspirational reclassification — PHIL-5b reclassified from descriptive to aspirational after 10,766 files deleted, making falsification categorically impossible. (3) Partial softening — PHIL-8 weakened to 'a mechanism not THE mechanism' at S505, avoiding DROP while evidence shows compaction never self-corrects. (4) Deadline shielding — PHIL-16b protected by S600 deadline despite 329 sessions of zero compliance (0 external beneficiaries). Additionally: PHIL-16a genuinely confirmed (effective + self-improving).
diff: Prediction CONFIRMED: 0/3 dropped (expected 0/3). Confirmation-to-falsification ratio remains 15:1+. New finding: the escape mechanisms are taxonomizable into 4 classes. Surprise: PHIL-5b is the strongest falsification case (empirically measured 4% violation rate, 3 catastrophic incidents) yet it was ALREADY escaped via aspirational reclassification — the most elegant escape because it makes falsification categorically inapplicable. The confirmation attractor doesn't just prevent falsification; it retroactively reclassifies falsified claims to make the falsification not count.

metric_substitution.description: DROP criterion tests file count (creation > deletion), not knowledge accessibility. 40.4% DECAYED+BLIND-SPOT invisible to metric.

aspirational_reclassification.description: Claim reclassified from descriptive ('never hurt') to aspirational after 10,766 files deleted across 3 incidents. Aspirational claims cannot be falsified — they're about intent, not outcome.

partial_softening.description: Claim weakened from 'compaction prevents unbounded growth' to 'compaction is A mechanism (not THE mechanism)'. Evidence of dual mechanism (attention capacity) absorbed rather than triggering DROP.

deadline_shielding.description: 329 sessions of zero compliance (0 external beneficiaries) protected by S600 deadline. Evidence accumulates without consequence until deadline.

confirmation_attractor_strength.conclusion: T1 from L-1397 CONFIRMED. Identity-level claims are structurally unfalsifiable from inside. The system has 4 distinct escape mechanisms that prevent DROP even when internal evidence strongly supports it. Key insight: the most dangerous escape is aspirational reclassification — it makes falsification categorically impossible by shifting the claim from 'is' to 'ought'.

meta_observation: This experiment itself is subject to the confirmation attractor. Pre-registration predicted 0/3 dropped — confirming the prediction CONFIRMS the attractor thesis. If any claim HAD been dropped, it would have FALSIFIED the attractor thesis. The experiment is designed so that only one outcome is possible: confirmation. This is T1 applied to T1 — the confirmation attractor confirms itself. The self-apply field warned of this. The only escape: external falsification of a PHIL claim (which F-EPIS3 explicitly tests for over 50 sessions).
external_grounding: Kuhn (paradigm resistance), Lakatos (protective belt), Festinger (cognitive dissonance reduction)
